frameworks.name: Bayesian feature inference with categorical prior
frameworks.one_line: Estimates emerge from combining sensory memory with a categorical prior downstream.

frameworks.name: Direct categorical modification of working memory
frameworks.one_line: Hypothesis that category labels rewrite sensory representations in memory.

frameworks.name: Selective memory recall / sample filtering
frameworks.one_line: Feedback gates which memory samples enter the inference process.

core_question: Do categorical judgments directly modify sensory representations in working memory, or do they bias estimates via downstream inference?
key_assumptions: Working memory holds a sensory representation that can be probed by estimation
Categorical prior is applied during feature inference, not at encoding
Subjects can flexibly switch categorical commitments given feedback
manipulations: Feedback forcing reversal of an initial categorical judgment before estimation
Comparison of estimates following correct vs. corrected category labels
key_findings: Estimates after reversal show no trace of the initial categorical judgment
Subjects flexibly adopt the corrected categorical prior for feature inference
Model comparison favors selective recall of memory samples consistent with the current category
interpretations: Categorical judgments act as top-down expectations on recall and inference, not as modifications of working memory contents
Categorical biases arise downstream from working memory via prior-based inference
